articles.title: Schools in Ganjam district closed for 3 days due to intense heatwave conditions - Kalinga TV
articles.search_term: heatwave (Angul OR Balangir OR Balasore OR Bargarh OR Bhadrak OR Boudh OR Cuttack OR Deogarh OR Dhenkanal OR Gajapati OR Ganjam OR Jagatsinghapur OR Jajpur OR Jharsuguda OR Kalahandi OR Kandhamal OR Kendrapara OR "Kendujhar (Keonjhar)" OR Khordha OR Koraput OR Malkangiri OR Mayurbhanj OR Nabarangpur OR Nayagarh OR Nuapada OR Puri OR Rayagada OR Sambalpur OR Sonepur OR Sundargarh) Odisha
articles.full_text:
Schools in Ganjam district closed for 3 days due to intense heatwave conditions
In view of the prevailing intense heatwave conditions, Ganjam district administration today announced closure of all schools across the district.
Berhampur: In view of the prevailing intense heatwave conditions, Ganjam district administration today announced closure of all schools across the district.
As per the announcement of the district administration, all government, government-aided and private schools in the district will remain closed for the students from April 21 to April 21.
The classes will temporarily remain suspended for all students from nursery to class 12. However, the previously scheduled supplementary examinations of class 5 and 8 will be held as per the schedule.
However, the District Education Officer (DEO) has informed that the administrative work and other essential work of the school will continue during this time.
Likewise, the Jagatsinghpur District administration also announced that all Government, Government Aided, and Privately Managed Schools shall function from 6.30 AM to 9 AM with immediate effect until further orders. The decision has been taken for the safety of the students amid the heatwave conditions. The administration also directed concerned officials to ensure adequate precautionary measures, including provision of safe drinking water, sufficient ORS packets and restriction of outdoor activities in schools during school hours.
Taking a serious note of the scorching heatwave and prediction of the India Meteorological Department (IMD) for further increase of temperature, around 14 districts also have announced closure of holidays for three days.

articles.title: Ganjam: Schools Closed for Students from April 21 to April 23 Amid Heatwave - News Room Odisha
articles.search_term: heatwave (Angul OR Balangir OR Balasore OR Bargarh OR Bhadrak OR Boudh OR Cuttack OR Deogarh OR Dhenkanal OR Gajapati OR Ganjam OR Jagatsinghapur OR Jajpur OR Jharsuguda OR Kalahandi OR Kandhamal OR Kendrapara OR "Kendujhar (Keonjhar)" OR Khordha OR Koraput OR Malkangiri OR Mayurbhanj OR Nabarangpur OR Nayagarh OR Nuapada OR Puri OR Rayagada OR Sambalpur OR Sonepur OR Sundargarh) Odisha
articles.full_text:
Berhampur: In the wake of prevailing hot and humid weather conditions, the Ganjam administration has announced temporary closure of schools for students across the district.
The decision has been taken to safeguard the health and well-being of children during the intense heat.
As per the order issued by the Collector and District Magistrate, all Government, Government-aided, and privately managed schools from Sishu Vatika (pre-primary) to Class XII will remain closed for students only from April 21 to April 23, 2026.
However, academic and administrative activities will not come to a halt. Teaching and non-teaching staff are required to attend school as usual, and essential functions such as admissions, administrative work, and census duties will continue without interruption.
Importantly, supplementary examinations for Class V and Class VIII will be conducted as per the schedule set by OSEPA, Bhubaneswar. School authorities have been directed to ensure proper arrangements, including adequate drinking water, ORS, and first-aid facilities, to ensure the safety of students appearing for exams.
The administration has urged schools to take all necessary precautions to minimize heat-related risks while ensuring continuity in essential academic processes.
